content: EF Core multi-tenancy with Global Query Filters uses a single ITenantService/ITenantProvider injected into DbContext constructor. The service resolves tenant ID from claims (or session/header). OnModelCreating applies HasQueryFilter per entity: .HasQueryFilter(e => e.TenantId == _tenantId). EF Core 10 adds named filters: HasQueryFilter("TenantIsolation", p => p.TenantId == _tenantId) enabling selective IgnoreQueryFilters(["SoftDelete"]) without bypassing tenant isolation. DbContext lifetime must be Scoped. SuperAdmin bypass uses .IgnoreQueryFilters() or IgnoreQueryFilters(["TenantIsolation"]) at query site. All tenant-scoped entities need a TenantId (GroupId) FK column. MS recommended approach: single shared database with discriminator column (TenantId) + global query filter.
